message:
beads: file 94bok for canonical-CBOR test drift + close 147fv

Filed flywheel_connectors-94bok [P1/HIGH] tracking the 5 pre-existing
fcp-auth-schema test failures caused by commit 6df66c79 switching
to_canonical_cbor to RFC 8949 byte-ordered map keys: the hand-rolled
CBOR test fixtures (decode_invalid_timestamp_reports_actual_value_not_zero,
decode_out_of_range_u16_yields_out_of_range_error_with_value,
decode_out_of_range_u64_preserves_value, type_mismatch_surfaces_helpful_error,
unknown_labels_are_ignored_on_decode) no longer round-trip because
ciborium::into_writer does not sort keys per RFC 8949.

Closes flywheel_connectors-147fv (separate commit 83f27e9a).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-8zp","title":"fcp.terraform: State inspection and resource listing","description":"# fcp.terraform: State inspection and resource listing (FCP2)

## Goal
Provide safe state inspection so agents can:
- list resources
- show a resource
- read outputs

State contains sensitive infrastructure details; all access must be audited.

## Scope
- List resources in state.
- Show a specific resource.
- Read outputs.
- Optional: pull remote state for inspection (read-only).

## Capability model (illustrative)
- `terraform.state.read` (sensitive)

## FCP2 requirements
- Single-zone binding.
- Sandbox profile must prevent arbitrary filesystem access.
- Audit:
  - every state read emits an audit event
- NetworkConstraints default deny unless backend requires explicit egress.

## Deliverables
- Operation schemas:
  - `terraform.state.list`
  - `terraform.state.show`
  - `terraform.output.read`
  - Optional: `terraform.state.pull`

## Test strategy (MUST)
- Unit tests with a terraform shim for:
  - correct arg formation
  - audit event emission
  - error mapping

## Acceptance criteria
- State access is safe, auditable, and bounded.

StateInspector with from_json(), list_resources(), list_resources_by_type/module(), show_resource(), read_outputs/output(), metadata(). Full audit event trail for every query. 126 tests, clippy clean.","created_at":"2026-03-09T19:05:54Z"}]}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-9095b","title":"[FCP3/P5.2] Implement lease, fencing, and coordinator semantics for multi node execution","description":"Background: multi node execution is unsafe without clear coordinator and fencing rules. The mesh must prevent split brain behavior where two nodes believe they own the same connector execution.

Scope: define and implement lease, fencing, and coordinator semantics in fcp-mesh, with durable records that survive process restarts and can be inspected later. Include the semantics for lease renewal, expiry, preemption, and explanation of why a node currently holds or lost authority. Also define the structured timeline events and authority records that later conformance and split brain tests must assert directly.

Acceptance: the platform has one clear story for exclusive ownership of active execution across nodes, with deterministic authority records and diagnostic traces that make split brain regressions obvious instead of mysterious.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"SilverBasin","created_at":"2026-03-27T18:32:07.204606Z","created_by":"CobaltHarbor","updated_at":"2026-04-02T20:58:11.210572Z","closed_at":"2026-04-02T20:09:29.000468Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["fcp3","fcp3-refoundation","fencing","leases","mesh-first","phase-5"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-9095b","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-ij290","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-27T18:32:39.131281Z","created_by":"CobaltHarbor","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":2263,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-9095b","author":"SilverBasin","text":"Implemented mesh-side authority inspection for lease/fencing work: added fcp-mesh AuthorityView/AuthorityRecord/AuthorityTimelineEvent, subject-bound singleton authority resolution in MeshNode planning, and direct authority assertions in the lease-contention conformance scenario. Local hygiene checks passed (rustfmt, git diff --check). Two explicit rch attempts were made after these edits: (1) from crates/fcp-mesh with 'rch exec -- cargo check --tests' and (2) from crates/fcp-conformance with 'rch exec -- cargo test --test integration_scenarios scenario_lease_contention -- --nocapture'. Both reached real remote cargo on worker vmi1149989 and failed immediately because that worker lacks nightly-2026-02-15-x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu; local cargo fallback was terminated immediately each time. Remaining gap is trusted remote cargo verification, not an observed repo compile error.","created_at":"2026-04-02T03:48:37Z"},{"id":2264,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-9095b","author":"jemanuel","text":"SunnyMoose claiming P5.2. Plan: implement lease acquisition/renewal/expiry/preemption in fcp-mesh with fencing tokens and coordinator election via HRW (rendezvous hashing). Will produce durable LeaseRecord and AuthorityTimeline types with structured diagnostic traces.","created_at":"2026-04-02T19:48:28Z"},{"id":2267,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-9095b","author":"SilverBasin","text":"Fresh-eyes audit found and fixed two correctness issues in the new mesh authority slice. (1) AuthorityView winner matching was too loose: duplicate observations from the same holder with the same fencing token but different expiry could both be marked Active. Fixed by matching the winner on holder + subject + purpose + fencing token + expires_at, and added a regression test for duplicate observations. (2) plan_execution() could still apply singleton-writer routing from an unrelated lease when singleton_writer=true but authority_subject was absent. Fixed by only enforcing singleton authority when the planner context binds a specific leased subject, and added a regression test covering the no-subject case. rustfmt and git diff --check passed after the fixes. rch verification was re-run with 'rch exec -- cargo check --tests' from crates/fcp-mesh and hit the same worker-image problem on vmi1149989 (missing nightly-2026-02-15-x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu); local fallback was terminated immediately.","created_at":"2026-04-02T19:52:29Z"},{"id":2269,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-9095b","author":"jemanuel","text":"SunnyMoose implemented LeaseCoordinator in fcp-mesh (commit 6cebeb56). Module covers: lease acquire/renew/release with monotonic fencing tokens, conflict detection with severity escalation, should_renew proactive renewal, configurable TTL bounds. 19 new tests, 1110/1110 mesh tests pass. Coordinator is stateless — computes authority from observed lease state.","created_at":"2026-04-02T20:09:19Z"},{"id":2287,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-9095b","author":"RubyCondor","text":"Fresh-eyes follow-up on the lease/coordinator slice: found and fixed one additional correctness bug in crates/fcp-mesh/src/coordinator.rs. `LeaseCoordinator::next_fencing_token()` previously incremented `next_seq` with plain `+= 1`, which could silently wrap in release builds after token exhaustion. It now uses `checked_add(1)` with the same explicit exhaustion behavior already used by the rebase path. Remote verification stayed rch-only and is now green after worker repair: from crates/fcp-mesh, `rch exec -- cargo test coordinator::tests:: --lib` passed on worker vmi1149989 (22/22 coordinator tests). This was a fresh-eyes correctness pass only; no change to issue ownership/status.","created_at":"2026-04-02T20:58:11Z"}]}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-90y","title":"fcp.semanticscholar: Research Literature Connector","description":"## Overview
Academic research connector for Semantic Scholar API, providing semantic paper search, citation analysis, and author profiling to complement the arxiv connector.

## Core Capabilities

### Paper Search with Semantic Understanding
- Natural language query support
- Semantic similarity matching (not just keywords)
- Field-of-study filtering
- Publication date range filtering
- Venue and journal filtering
- Open access filter option

### Citation Graph and References
- Retrieve citing papers (who cited this)
- Retrieve referenced papers (bibliography)
- Citation context extraction
- Multi-hop citation traversal
- Co-citation analysis
- Bibliographic coupling detection

### Author Profiles and Metrics
- Author search by name and affiliation
- H-index and citation count retrieval
- Publication history timeline
- Co-author network mapping
- Author disambiguation
- ORCID linking where available

### Topic Classification
- Automatic topic/field assignment
- Hierarchical topic taxonomy
- Cross-disciplinary paper detection
- Emerging topic identification
- Topic trend analysis over time

### Open Access Paper Retrieval
- Identify open access versions
- Direct PDF URL when available
- Preprint linking (arxiv, bioRxiv, etc.)
- License information extraction
- Full text availability status

## Integration with Arxiv Connector
- Cross-reference arxiv IDs with S2 paper IDs
- Enrich arxiv results with citation data
- Use S2 for citation metrics, arxiv for full text
- Unified paper identifier mapping

## Implementation Notes
- Use Semantic Scholar Academic Graph API
- Respect rate limits (100 req/5min for unauthenticated)
- API key support for higher limits
- Batch request support for efficiency
- Handle partial data gracefully

## Testing Requirements
- Mock API responses for unit tests
- Rate limit handling tests
- Citation graph traversal tests
- Search relevance validation","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-11T15:21:50.282684625Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-15T08:47:46.730657198Z","closed_at":"2026-01-15T08:47:46.730657198Z","close_reason":"Duplicate of fcp.semanticscholar connector bead (flywheel_connectors-ixwa)","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-90y","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-1n78.20","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T18:31:41Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
+{"id":"flywheel_connectors-94bok","title":"[REVIEW][HIGH] fcp-auth-schema 5 tests broken by canonical CBOR ordering switch in 6df66c79","description":"crates/fcp-auth-schema/src/claims.rs — after commit 6df66c79 switched to_canonical_cbor/from_canonical_cbor to RFC 8949 byte-ordered map keys, 5 hand-rolled CBOR test inputs now fail from_canonical_cbor with Decode("non-canonical CBOR encoding") because they either (a) do not sort keys, (b) sort by i64/i128 numeric rather than RFC 8949 encoded-byte order. Failing tests: decode_invalid_timestamp_reports_actual_value_not_zero, decode_out_of_range_u16_yields_out_of_range_error_with_value, decode_out_of_range_u64_preserves_value, type_mismatch_surfaces_helpful_error, unknown_labels_are_ignored_on_decode. Repro: CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/fcp-swarm-p2 cargo test --offline -p fcp-auth-schema -> 5 failed, 16 passed. Suggested fix: have each test build its CBOR bytes via fcp_cbor::to_canonical_cbor(&ciborium::Value::Map(entries)) (already a workspace dep of fcp-auth-schema post-6df66c79) instead of manual ciborium::into_writer. Reviewer: cc_1.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-23T18:35:08.360288Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-23T18:35:08.360288Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-97lx","title":"[LC] Tests: unit + integration","description":"# [LC] Tests: unit + integration

## Goal
Validate rollout logic, rollback triggers, and pinning behavior.

## Unit Tests
- Canary success -> full rollout.
- Canary failure -> rollback with audit.
- Pin prevents auto-upgrade.

## Integration Tests
- Simulated connector health regression triggers rollback.

## Logging Requirements
- Structured JSON logs per transition (state_from, state_to, reason, duration_ms).

## Acceptance Criteria
- Deterministic logs and stable state transitions.","notes":"[AlienArtifactPractical]
- Add metamorphic tests (encode->decode->encode) plus differential oracle for edge cases.
- Use sequential test budgets with e-value thresholds to stop early when safe.
[ExtremeOptimizationPractical]
- Parallelize vector generation with deterministic seeds.
- Cache canonical forms to avoid repeated parse cost.
[AlienArtifactReview] Updated 2026-02-02T23:58:59.240755+00:00","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"GoldOx","created_at":"2026-02-01T03:03:57.847574850Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-09T07:06:11.963332Z","closed_at":"2026-03-09T07:06:11.963308Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-97lx","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-334n","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-02T18:31:41Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-97lx","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-3fdt","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T18:31:41Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-97lx","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-s9t8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T18:31:41Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":991,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-97lx","author":"Jeff Emanuel","text":"SunnyMoose: The 48 rollout unit tests in fcp-host/src/rollout.rs already cover all required scenarios:
- Canary success -> full rollout: tests promote_success, ctrl_schedule_from_production
- Canary failure -> rollback with audit: tests eval_crash_loop, eval_error_rate, failure_streak_triggers_rollback, ctrl_unavailable_rolls_back
- Pin prevents auto-upgrade: test eval_pinned
- Structured JSON logs: evidence bundles are deterministic and serializable (evidence_roundtrip, audit_event_digest_and_roundtrip)
Additional supply_chain module adds 45 tests for verification gate (policy enforcement, caching, determinism).
Total fcp-host unit tests: 588, all pass.","created_at":"2026-03-07T16:39:42Z"},{"id":1018,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-97lx","author":"GoldOx","text":"Added rollout rollback/logging integration coverage in crates/fcp-host/tests/host_connector_integration.rs and cleaned crates/fcp-host/src/batch.rs so current fcp-host verification stays green. New coverage exercises canary scheduling -> failed evaluation -> automatic rollback, asserts lifecycle state becomes RolledBack, and verifies structured endpoint plus rollout-decision JSON logs (including decision, state_before/state_after, and evidence_digest). Verification completed with: rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=.target-goldox-host-cache cargo clippy -p fcp-host --all-targets -- -D warnings; rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=.target-goldox-host-cache cargo test -p fcp-host --all-targets; rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=.target-goldox-host-cache cargo check --workspace --all-targets; rustfmt --edition 2024 --check on the touched fcp-host files.","created_at":"2026-03-07T20:56:26Z"},{"id":1156,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-97lx","author":"Jeff Emanuel","text":"SunnyMoose: Verified all acceptance criteria met. 126 rollout tests pass (canary success/failure, rollback audit, pin prevents auto-upgrade, structured JSON logs). GoldOx added integration coverage for canary->failure->rollback lifecycle. Closing.","created_at":"2026-03-09T07:06:05Z"}]}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-98w","title":"Add message length validation","description":"Add validation for message content length before sending to APIs. Discord limit: 2000 chars, Telegram limit: 4096 chars. Return clear FCP error if exceeded.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-11T14:38:31.560402709Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-11T15:03:10.668752876Z","closed_at":"2026-01-11T15:03:10.668752876Z","close_reason":"Added message length validation for both connectors: Discord (2000 chars content, embed limits) and Telegram (4096 chars). Validation now happens before config check for better error messages. Added 8 unit tests.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-991m3","title":"Anthropic: update API_VERSION from 2023-06-01 to current stable, make configurable","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:09:16.493442Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-03-25T03:54:52.379625Z","closed_at":"2026-03-25T03:54:52.379408Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["api-versions","connectors","hardening"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-991m3","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-kn1et","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:09:27.886898Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1947,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-991m3","author":"jemanuel","text":"File: connectors/anthropic/src/client.rs:30
Current: const API_VERSION: &str = "2023-06-01";
Used at: lines 200, 349, 383, 621, 775 (sent as 'anthropic-version' header on every request)

The Anthropic API version header determines which response format and feature set is available. Version 2023-06-01 was the initial public API version. Newer versions add features like tool_use, image inputs, extended thinking, and different response schemas.

Steps:
1. Check https://docs.anthropic.com/en/api/versioning for the current stable version
2. Update the const to the latest stable version
3. Add api_version field to the connector's configure() config struct
4. In client construction, prefer config.api_version over the compiled default
5. Support FCP_ANTHROPIC_API_VERSION environment variable as fallback override
6. Add the active API version to doctor() output
7. Update all wiremock matchers in tests that assert the version header value
8. Verify streaming (SSE) endpoint also uses the correct version header","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:09:27Z"},{"id":1970,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-991m3","author":"jemanuel","text":"SunnyMoose completed: Added resolve_api_version() with hierarchy: config override > FCP_ANTHROPIC_API_VERSION env var > compiled default (2023-06-01). Added api_version field to AnthropicClient struct. Added new_with_auth_and_version() constructor. Updated all 3 request-building methods to use self.api_version. Test wiremock matchers use DEFAULT_API_VERSION. 286 tests pass.","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:54:52Z"}]}
